summary: High‑energy 1980 show captured on a clean soundboard tape, highlighted by soaring Sugaree and a stellar Feel Like a Stranger/Play‑in‑the‑Band second set
review: The concert is widely praised as one of the best from June ‘80, with tight playing, youthful energy and numerous standout moments – especially Sugaree, the up‑tempo Feel Like a Stranger close, Play in the Band’s orbital jam and an inspired Terrapin Station. The Miller soundboard source delivers a clear, hiss‑free mix with only minor splice patches that don’t detract from the listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 5) and enthusiastic comments about both performance and sound quality; recording is clean, show is described as one of the best of the year.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, hiss‑free mix with Garcia front‑and‑center. Minor splice/patches noted but well‑executed and not disruptive.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as loose but adventurous; second set praised for seamless transitions, high‑energy jams and a powerful climax that flows naturally into the encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly lauded – crisp early, gain‑rich during jams, psychedelic on PITB. One reviewer jokes he sounds “soupy,” but overall his playing is highlighted as a major strength.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work noted as “smart bombs” and “funky”; despite occasional 1980 uncertainty, reviewers feel Phil locks in the groove especially in the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives abundant praise for inventive fills, groovy comping and leading sections; his keyboards are called “alive” throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers (Bill & Mickey) are described as motivated, vying for the top spot and driving the high‑octane jams.
